_comment: Schema for comms/claude_trade_scores.jsonl — the durable, repo-tracked log of Claude's per-trade decision scores produced by .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md. APPEND-ONLY JSONL: one JSON object per graded trade, keyed by `trade_id` so it cross-references trade_journal.db::trades. The file may carry a single leading {"_meta": ...} line (skip rows without `trade_id`). This is the Tier-1 repo-artifact persistence path (operator decision 2026-05-24): scores live here now and are ingestible by the trainer (the repo is git-pulled onto the trainer VM each cycle). PLANNED Tier-2 follow-up: ingest these rows into their OWN table (`claude_trade_scores`, separate from `trades`) in the canonical store, cross-referenced by trade_id, and surface the score alongside trade data in the dashboard app. `decision_grade_score` mirrors the review_journal family mapping (A/B/C/D/F -> 4/3/2/1/0) so that ingestion is a straight column copy. This file is documentation only — it is not validated as a JSON Schema.

record.reviewed_at: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp — when Claude graded this trade
record.review_window: since-last-review window this grade came from, e.g. '2026-05-22T00:00Z..2026-05-24T00:00Z'
record.setup: vwap | turtle_soup | ict_scalp_5m | trend_donchian | fade_breakout_4h | squeeze_breakout_4h | ...
record.decision_grade_score: 4 | 3 | 2 | 1 | 0 — numeric mirror of decision_grade (A=4..F=0), matches the review_journal dataset family
record.entry_quality: optimal | acceptable | late | early | should_skip | unknown
record.exit_quality: optimal | tp_appropriate | sl_appropriate | premature_exit | held_too_long | unknown
record.risk_management: correct | oversize | undersize | sl_too_tight | sl_too_wide | unknown
record.rationale: <= 240 chars — why this grade given the trade's signal_logic + outcome
record.alternative_action: <= 160 chars — what we'd do differently next time, or 'none'
